data.title: Intel said to be nearing $1.6B deal to buy AI chip startup SambaNova
data.description:
Intel (INTC) is in advanced discussion with privately owned SambaNova Systems Inc. to acquire the artificial intelligence chip startup for roughly $1.6B, including debt, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The parties could reach a buyout agreement as early as January, the people said, asking for anonymity because the information is not yet public. 

Terms and timing of the deal could change, according to the people, who added that SambaNova, which has signed term sheets with other potential investors, could seek another strategic direction.

Intel (INTC) CEO Lip-Bu Tan serves as chairman of Palo Alto, California-based SambaNova, which designs custom AI chips in a market dominated by Nvidia (NVDA).

A potential acquisition is expected to give Intel (INTC) a much sought-after platform to help build out its AI portfolio at a discount, after SambaNova drew a $5B valuation in 2021 as part of a funding round led by SoftBank Group’s (SFTBY) (SFTBF) Vision Fund 2.

data.title: House Republicans introduce health care bill without enhanced subsidies
data.description:
A Republican health care bill introduced in the House Friday evening would not extend expiring enhanced ACA subsidies -- a measure being demanded by Democrats  -- but instead allow employers to work with others across industries to purchase group health plans together and mandate greater transparency from pharmacy benefit managers, the so-called "drug middlemen."

GOP leaders, in a concession to moderates in its party, will allow a floor vote to extend the enhanced subsidies, Politico reported.

The new legislation, dubbed The Lower Health Care Premium for All Americans Act and introduced by Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa), would provide funding for cost sharing reduction staring in 2027, "lowering premiums and stabilizing the individual market while ensuring taxpayer dollars are used responsibly," a news release from Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) stated.

On the PBM front, the bill would require them to provide employers with data on prescription drug spending, rebates, spread pricing, and formulary choices.

Accessibility to Association Health Plans would also increase. Currently, these plans are only available to employers in the same trade, industry, or profession, with no options for the self-employed. The legislation would change this by allowing employers irrespective of industry or profession to join together, and also allow the self-employed to take part. 

In addition, it would formalize a concept known as Custom Health Option and Individual Care Expense (CHOICE) arrangement permiting employers to give money to employees to purchase their own coverage and pay those premiums pre-tax.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Enova, eToro, NP, SLM among week's biggest financials movers
data.description:
Financial stocks (XLF) largely drifted higher, while the broader S&P 500 slipped in the past week, which was marked by the Federal Reserve's third consecutive interest-rate cut.

Enova International (ENVA) rose the most of any financial stock (with market cap over $2B) in the week ended Dec. 12, 2025, jumping 20% after announcing a $369M deal to buy Grasshopper Bancorp;

Neptune Insurance Holdings (NP) gapped up 12%;

Slide Insurance Holdings (SLDE) climbed 11%;

American International Group (AIG) gained 10%; and

HCI Group (HCI) rounded out the five biggest winners -- mostly comprising insurance companies -- with a 10.2% rise.

On the losers, Sallie Mae (SLM) retreated 12% after the student debt lender introduced below-consensus earnings estimates during its 2025 investor day forum after Monday's close;

eToro Group (ETOR) slid 11.8%;

Cipher Mining (CIFR) shed 11.6%;

IREN (IREN) fell 10%; and

Robinhood Markets (HOOD), which during the week posted disappointing trading volumes for November, gapped down 9.4%.

data.title: SpaceX valued at $800B in share sale ahead of potential 2026 IPO
data.description:
Ahead of a possible IPO in 2026, billionaire Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite maker SpaceX (SPACE) has commenced a secondary share sale that would value the company at $800B, Reuters reported, citing a shareholder sent by CFO Bret Johnsen.

In a letter dated Dec. 12, Johnsen confirmed plans for the company’s IPO and announced an arrangement with new and existing investors to buy SpaceX (SPACE) shares from eligible shareholders at $421 apiece.

“We are preparing the company for a possible IPO in 2026,” he said, confirming CEO Musk’s prior remarks about the public listing.

“Whether it actually happens, when it happens, and at what valuation are still highly uncertain, but the thinking is that if we execute brilliantly and the markets cooperate, a public offering could raise a significant amount of capital,” Johnsen added.

The IPO, which could rank among the biggest public listings in the world, is expected to raise funds for the company’s plans to establish AI data centers in space, send uncrewed and crewed missions to Mars, and other ambitions, according to him.

data.title: Apollo reportedly bet against debt of software makers exposed to AI
data.description:
Apollo Global Management (APO) has taken bearish bets on several large loans held by software makers vulnerable to artificial intelligence and reduced its credit exposure to the sector, The Financial Times reported on Saturday.

Companies including Internet Brands, SonicWall, and Perforce, owned by large private equity firms KKR (KKR), Francisco Partners, and Clearlake, respectively, were subject to APO’s bearish bets, according to people briefed on the matter.

The bets made up less than 1% of the private capital giant’s overall $700B in credit assets, one person said, while another added that they lasted through much of 2025 but have since been closed.

It was not immediately clear the exact size and the returns of the short positions, and having sold off several times this year, the loans Apollo (APO) bet against are now trading above 80 cents on the dollar.

According to the FT, the company has previously determined AI as a potential opportunity for software companies, with many of its private credit funds holding about 20% of exposure to the sector at the start of this year.

Since then, Apolo (APO) has cut its investments in the sector by almost half, with its top leadership recommending against directional industry bets and deciding to reduce exposure.

“Technology change is going to cause massive dislocation in the credit market,” CEO Marc Rowan said at a recent conference. “I don’t know whether that’s going to be enterprise software, which could […] benefit or be destroyed by this. As a lender, I’m not sure I want to be there to find out.”

data.title: Zscaler, Netskope among BTIG's top picks in security, infrastructure software stocks heading into 2026
data.description:
BTIG's top large-cap pick in security and infrastructure software stocks for the first half of 2026 is Zscaler (ZS), while the top small-and-mid-cap pick is Netskope (NTSK)

The firm's other favored names include Snowflake (SNOW), SailPoint (SAIL) and Datadog (DDOG).

BTIG provided a review of 2025 and an outlook for 2026, highlighting key themes across its security and infrastructure software coverage.

"On the security front, we believe demand has stabilized, with the companies we cover set to sustain 15%–16% revenue growth next year, similar to 2025. Securing AI will likely remain a central theme, but we expect buyers to prioritize existing vendor capabilities before committing significant budgets to emerging, AI-specific security solutions. This transition will happen over time. But we think it will be gradual," said analysts led by Gray Powell.

The analysts also expect continued emphasis on platform adoption, with companies offeringbest-in-class products and expanding functionality within current buying centers and adjacent markets likely to outperform. On the observability side, the analysts expect customers to focus on vendor consolidation to streamline operations, and see more tailwinds from AI adoption than risk.

2025 Review

"It has been a rough year for our security and infrastructure software coverage. Despite stable top line performance, the group lagged major indices meaningfully. The YTD median return for the group is currently -0.8% versus a 22% gain in the Nasdaq," said Powell and his team.

The analysts noted that the top five performers were Cloudflare (NET), MongoDB (MDB), CrowdStrike (CRWD), Snowflake and CyberArk Software (CYBR). All are expected to benefit from AI in varying forms. Bottom performers this year can generally be categorized as point solutions in segments being commoditized by larger platform vendors and/or seeing headwinds related to AI, according to the analysts.

The analysts added that, as bad as the stock performance was this year, operational results were good. The typical company saw out-year revenue estimates revised higher by over 1%. The biggest upward revision to 2026 revenue forecasts came at Snowflake (+6.3%), while Okta (OKTA) and Elastic (ESTC) were tied for second (+3.7% each). The biggest downside revisions came at OneSpan (OSPN) (-11.4%), Rapid7 (RPD) (-7.0%) and SentinelOne (S) (-5.2%), according to the analysts.

Outlook 2026

"Cyber security growth has been stabilizing over the course of 2025. The companies we cover are on-track to grow at a 16% pace this year, and we expect similar growth in 2026. We remain most optimistic about cloud security, Secure Service Edge/Zero Trust, and identity. In addition, we see agrowing opportunity for vendors to disrupt the SIEM [Security Information and Event Management] space," said Powell and his team.

The analysts noted that while AI security is a major emerging theme, they expect most organizations will stick with current vendor solutions to protect AI workloads over the next 12 to 18 months.

BTIG's top AI exposure names include Netskope, SailPoint, Cloudflare, Zscaler, Palo Alto Networks (PANW) and CrowdStrike.

"Platform consolidation also remains a top theme. But in our fieldwork, skepticism persists over whether any vendor can truly offer best-in-class products across multiple security categories. We favor companies with strong multi-product offerings, especially those able to drive adoption within the same or adjacent buying centers," said the analysts.

The analysts think CrowdStrike, Zscaler, and Palo Alto Networks play best on the platform theme.

"We remain positive on the observability space. We see customers prioritizing vendor consolidation, benefiting both DDOG and Dynatrace (DT). Vendors should see tailwinds from the proliferation of workloads from AI," said Powell and his team.

The analysts noted that Palo Alto's entry into observability with the announced acquisition of Chronosphere introduces a new potential disruptive catalyst to the market. However, the analysts added that they have seen Palo Alto's price aggressively in other verticals, like in endpointsecurity. In that scenario, there was little impact to the market leader CrowdStrike. "In the case of observability, we see similar dynamics with DDOG and DT," said Powell and his team.

data.title: Lululemon, Biogen to exit Nasdaq 100 with Alnylam, Seagate among additions
data.description:
Six companies, including Alnylam Pharmaceuticals (ALNY) and Seagate Technology Holdings (STX), are set to join the Nasdaq 100 Index (NDX) as part of the benchmark’s annual reconstitution this month, with Biogen (BIIB) and Lululemon Athletica (LULU) among notable exclusions.

The Nasdaq-100, which represents the biggest non-financial stocks listed on the Nasdaq, undergoes its annual reconstitution in December, with companies meeting an average daily trading volume of at least 200,000 shares and other criteria considered for inclusion.

The event marks a pivotal moment for the new constituents, as many large index funds, such as the $409B Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 ETF (QQQ), which track the Nasdaq 100, must own all of the gauge’s components.

As part of this year’s shakeup, the benchmark’s overseer will remove CDW Corporation (CDW), GlobalFoundries (GFS), ON Semiconductor Corporation (ON), and The Trade Desk (TTD), in addition to Biogen (BIIB) and Lululemon Athletica (LULU).

They will be replaced by Ferrovial SE (FER), Insmed Incorporated (INSM), Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR), Western Digital (WDC), Alnylam (ALNY), and Seagate (STX).

The changes are set to take effect before the market opens on Dec. 22.

data.title: Trump warns of land strikes in Latin America over drugs
data.description:
President Donald Trump said Friday that the United States is preparing to expand its campaign against drug trafficking by moving from maritime operations to strikes on land in Latin America.

The warning comes as oil markets remain on alert for signs that tensions with Venezuela could turn into broader hostilities, a development that could threaten crude supplies from the OPEC member. Venezuela produced roughly 920,000 barrels of oil a day in November, a modest recovery from recent lows after years of sanctions and underinvestment.

The president on Friday again stopped short of specifying when the shift would occur, where it would take place or whether regional governments still have options to head off such action.

“We knocked out 96% of the drugs coming in by water, and now we’re starting by land, and by land is a lot easier, and that’s going to start happening,” Trump told reporters during an Oval Office appearance.

The remarks build on several days of warnings from the president, who has been vowing to widen a military effort that has so far focused on destroying what the Pentagon describes as drug-smuggling vessels operating in international waters off South America.

Although many observers see Trump’s threats as aimed principally at Venezuela’s Nicolás Maduro, the president suggested the impacts may extend beyond that country. “It doesn’t necessarily have to be in Venezuela,” he said, adding that “people that are bringing in drugs to our country are targets.”

Trump has defended the campaign by likening drug interdiction to a form of warfare, arguing that if overdose fatalities were tallied the way military deaths are, the nation would be “like a war that would be unparalleled.”

A move to attack targets on land would mark a significant escalation. Earlier in the week, Maduro warned that if Venezuela were subjected to foreign aggression, workers should launch a “general insurrectionary strike” and push for “an even more radical revolution.”

data.title: Critical One Energy plans flow-through share offering of up to CDN$1 mln
data.description:
• Critical One Energy (CSE: CRTL) (OTCQB: MMTLF) said on Friday it intends to raise up to CDN$1,00,000 through a non-brokered private placement of up to 1,000,000 flow-through shares priced at CDN$1.00 each.

  • The company may pay eligible finders a cash fee of up to 6% and finder warrants equal to up to 6% of the flow-through shares issued.

  • Each warrant will be exercisable at CDN$1.50 for 18 months after closing.

  • Proceeds will be used for eligible Canadian exploration expenses qualifying as flow-through critical mineral mining expenditures.

  • The placement is expected to close on or about December 19, 2025, with securities subject to a four-month-and-one-day hold period.

  • MMTLF closed +0.25% at $0.8.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lennar tops list of most shorted S&P 500 consumer discretionary stocks in November; Amazon remains least shorted
data.description:
Average short interest across the S&P 500 consumer discretionary stocks was 2.57% at the end of November, Lennar (LEN) being the most shorted stock in the sector and Amazon.com (AMZN) as the least shorted in the sector.

Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLY) has been up 7.63% so far this year, as compared to the over 16.08% rise in the broader S&P 500 index over the same period.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

Lennar (LEN) was the most shorted stock at 16.24% of shares float, followed by MGM Resorts International (NASDAQ:MGM) and Pool (NASDAQ:POOL) with short interest of 11.21% and 8.17%, respectively.

Bets against Broadline Retail, represented by Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN) remained the least shorted stock, with 73.61M shares sold short, or 0.76% of the shares float, followed by The Home Depot (NYSE:HD) and McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) with short interest of 0.92% and 0.97%, respectively.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

Homebuilding remained the most shorted industry within the sector, that surged to 7.55% at the end of November from 4.48% in October.

Home Furnishing Retail stood next at 6.32%, up from 5.64% last month, followed by Other Speciality Retail at 5.98% slightly edging up from 5.97% in the end of October. 

Broadline Retail, which includes Amazon, and Home Improvement Retail remained among the lowest within the sector, at 0.79% and 1.22%, respectively.

Overall, short positioning across the consumer discretionary sector remained broadly stable in November, despite pockets of increased short interest in select industries.
